ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for organic brewed iced tea; SNOMED CT coverage for beverage subtypes is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving is one cup (approximately 240 ml), equivalent to a typical glass of iced tea served at a café or hawker stall.

nutrient_source.title: Organic Brewed Iced Tea — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Organic Brewed Iced Tea (cr_id G00010128). All per-100g values preserved from the canonical HPB record. Saturated fat not reported by HPB; set to 0.0 as this is a tea-based beverage with negligible fat content (total fat 0.15 g/100g). Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat set to -1 as HPB does not report fatty acid fractions for this beverage.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for organic brewed iced tea with 8.43 g sugar/100g. GI estimated at 56 (medium) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened iced tea / sugar-sweetened tea beverages, which report GI values in the range 47–68 depending on sugar concentration. A midpoint of 56 is used as the best defensible analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (56 × 20.8) / 100 ≈ 11.6, rounded to 12.0. Serving size used: 240 g × 8.66 g carbohydrate/100g = 20.8 g carbohydrate per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (8.43 g/100g) in liquid form; sugar-sweetened beverages independently raise blood pressure risk regardless of low sodium
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this sweetened iced tea; the 8.43 g sugar per 100 ml exceeds the 5 g/100 ml SSB threshold and sugar-sweetened beverages independently elevate blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines — request unsweetened brewed tea instead.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 8.43 g sugar/100g and zero dietary fibre; liquid sugar causes rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this beverage as the 8.43 g sugar per 100 ml with zero fibre constitutes a sugar-sweetened beverage that causes rapid blood glucose spikes — unsweetened brewed tea is the diabetes-appropriate alternative.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with increased cardiovascular risk; sodium is low (16 mg/100g) but sugar load is clinically significant
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit or avoid this sweetened iced tea; while sodium is low at 16 mg/100g, the 8.43 g sugar per 100 ml contributes to excess caloric intake and is associated with dyslipidaemia and elevated cardiovascular risk per AHA dietary guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar (8.43 g/100g) promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; no anti-inflammatory nutrients present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this sweetened iced tea as the high sugar content promotes inflammatory mediators and the beverage contains no anti-inflammatory factors — unsweetened green or black tea with polyphenols is the RA-appropriate alternative.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Organic Brewed Iced Tea is a chilled tea beverage made by brewing organic black tea leaves in hot water and serving the resulting infusion over ice. Despite the 'organic' labelling and the description 'unsweetened, brewed fresh daily', the nutritional profile recorded in the HPB Singapore Foodo database shows 8.43 g of sugar per 100 g, which classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) by standard public health definitions. The beverage contains 107 kcal per 100 g, 8.66 g carbohydrate, 0.15 g fat, and only 0.01 g protein. Dietary fibre is 0 g and potassium is very low at 7 mg per 100 g. Sodium is 16 mg per 100 g. The high sugar content in liquid form is clinically significant because liquid sugars are absorbed more rapidly than solid food sugars, causing faster postprandial blood glucose elevation. This makes the beverage unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and rheumatoid arthritis. The glycemic index is estimated at 56 (medium) based on analogous sweetened tea beverage data from the University of Sydney GI Database, with a glycemic load of approximately 12 per standard 240 ml serving. Unsweetened brewed tea would be a clinically preferable alternative for all three conditions. The beverage is vegan, gluten-free, dairy-free, and halal-suitable. In Singapore it is commonly served at cafés and hawker stalls and is known in Chinese as 有机冲泡冰茶 and in Malay as Teh Ais Organik Seduh.
common_user_queries: Is brewed iced tea good for hypertension?
How much sugar is in iced tea?
Calories in iced tea per cup
Is iced tea suitable for rheumatoid arthritis?
Is sweetened iced tea bad for blood pressure?
Organic tea vs regular tea nutrition

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry (cr_id G00010128, confidence 0.95). All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database, which is the approved GI source. Multilingual names added for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to satisfy G6 compliance for a Singapore-served beverage.
notes: 1. Negative sentinel values: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 because the HPB Singapore Foodo database does not report these values for this beverage entry. 2. Saturated fat is set to 0.0 as total fat is only 0.15 g/100g in a tea-based beverage; fatty acid fractions are not reported by HPB and saturated fat is confirmed negligible. 3. Magnesium is reported as 0.0 mg/100g per the HPB source (confirmed zero). 4. Dietary fibre is 0.0 g/100g per HPB (confirmed zero for this brewed tea beverage). 5. Cholesterol is 0.0 mg/100g — naturally zero as this is a plant-based tea beverage with no animal-derived ingredients. 6. Protein_g (0.01 g/100g) is trace-level and not zero; fat_g (0.15 g/100g) is trace-level and not zero — no zero-macro justification required for these fields. 7. The description in the input record states 'Unsweetened, brewed fresh daily', which conflicts with the HPB-reported sugar content of 8.43 g/100g. The HPB nutritional data is treated as authoritative; the description may reflect the preparation intent or a labelling discrepancy. 8. GI is an analogous estimate (GI 56, medium) based on sweetened tea beverage data from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in approved sources. 9. Although the input record classifies this as Western cuisine, the beverage is served in Singapore and multilingual names have been added for Chinese (有机冲泡冰茶 / 有機沖泡冰茶), Malay (Teh Ais Organik Seduh), Tamil (ஆர்கானிக் குளிர் தேநீர்), and Hindi (ऑर்गेनिக் ब्रूड आइस्ड टी) to satisfy G6 multilingual naming compliance for Singapore-served foods.
